title: aethionema saxatile var. thomasianum
intro: Aethionema saxatile var. thomasianum, also known as Thomas' rock jasmine, is a flowering plant species belonging to the Brassicaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is believed to offer health benefits, including reducing fever and soothing irritated throats, which can be attributed to its antipyretic and mucolytic properties. Herbal preparations of Thomas' rock jasmine include infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate respiratory symptoms and promote overall well-being.
